untranslatable_reason: Broadening Top pattern requires detection of two sloping trend lines (one upward, one downward) with multiple touches and formation of higher highs/lower lows. This requires trend line detection and multi-point intersection logic that cannot be expressed using only point-in-time indicator values and basic comparison operators. Pattern recognition of this complexity requires custom pattern detection logic beyond the scope of indicator-based rules.
translation_notes: Entry conditions attempt to capture the core characteristic of expanding range (higher highs and lower lows) but cannot properly encode the full megaphone geometry with dual trend lines. Exit condition uses mean reversion to SMA as a reasonable proxy for breakout failure/pullback given author silence on precise stop and target calculations. Success rate of 53% in bear markets and 15% average decline noted in parameters but not directly translatable to rules.
